name: it-services
title: IT Services & Delivery
industry: Managed services, IT consulting, platform engineering
description: Adapts the harness to produce IT-services delivery artifacts — statements of work, service designs, and runbooks — where the failure mode is an ambiguous scope boundary, an unmeasurable SLA, or a runbook with no rollback, not a failing build.
deliverableKind: Statement of work / service design & runbook

stages.label: Frame
stages.purpose: Define the client need, the in-scope and out-of-scope boundaries, and the constraints (budget, timeline, environments, compliance).
stages.output: Scope note: client need, in/out-of-scope, constraints

stages.label: Design
stages.purpose: State the target architecture, the SLA targets, and the operating model before any runbook is written. The plan the draft must follow.

stages.label: Draft
stages.purpose: Write the SOW / service design / runbook: architecture, SLA targets, operating procedures, rollback — each claim sourced, each target measurable.

stages.label: Review breadth
stages.purpose: Scan every section for completeness and internal consistency; flag missing sections, gates without verdicts, unsubstantiated claims.

stages.label: Review depth
stages.purpose: Run the gate verdicts: scope clarity, SLA measurability, operational readiness, security & compliance, cost/effort traceability.

stages.label: Feedback
stages.purpose: Evaluate reviewer challenges, reconcile scope against cost, finalize the go-live readiness sign-off.

gates.name: Scope Clarity
gates.question: Are the in-scope and out-of-scope boundaries explicit, so no work is assumed by either party that was never agreed?

gates.name: SLA Measurability
gates.question: Is every SLA target quantified and measurable (a number, a window, a metric) rather than a vague aspiration?

gates.name: Operational Readiness
gates.question: Are the runbook and rollback complete, and have the go-live readiness gates each been signed off with a verdict?

gates.name: Security & Compliance
gates.question: Are access control, data handling, and audit requirements addressed and specific to this engagement?

gates.name: Cost/Effort Traceability
gates.question: Does the stated cost or effort trace to the scoped work, with reusable components factored out rather than re-priced per engagement?
